title: Quiz: Binomial Tree Option Pricing
description: Test your understanding of binomial tree methods for option pricing

questions.question: In the Cox-Ross-Rubinstein model, what is the relationship between up factor u and down factor d?
questions.explanation: In the CRR model, d = 1/u, ensuring the tree recombines (an up followed by down returns to the original price). This also ensures the lattice is symmetric around the initial price.

questions.question: What does the risk-neutral probability p represent in binomial pricing?

options.text: The actual probability of the stock going up
The probability that makes the expected return equal to the risk-free rate
The probability of exercising the option
The probability of the option expiring in the money

questions.explanation: The risk-neutral probability p is chosen so that the expected stock return equals the risk-free rate. This allows us to price derivatives by discounting expected payoffs at the risk-free rate.

questions.question: For a non-dividend paying stock, when should you early exercise an American call option?

options.text: When the option is deep in the money
Never - early exercise is never optimal

questions.explanation: For non-dividend paying stocks, it's never optimal to exercise an American call early. The call is worth more alive due to time value and the benefit of delaying the strike payment.

questions.question: How does the binomial tree price compare to Black-Scholes as the number of steps N increases?

options.text: It converges to Black-Scholes at rate O(1/N)
It converges to Black-Scholes at rate O(1/N²)
It equals Black-Scholes exactly for any N

questions.explanation: The binomial tree price converges to the Black-Scholes price at rate O(1/N). This means doubling the number of steps roughly halves the error.

questions.question: American put options are always worth at least as much as European puts.
questions.explanation: True. American puts can do everything European puts can do, plus they offer the additional flexibility of early exercise. This extra optionality can never decrease value.

questions.question: In backward induction for American options, at each node we take:

options.text: The maximum of continuation and exercise values
The average of continuation and exercise values

questions.explanation: At each node, we take the maximum of the continuation value (holding the option) and the immediate exercise value. The holder will exercise if it's optimal to do so.

questions.question: What happens to the early exercise premium of an American put as the stock price decreases?
questions.explanation: As the stock price falls deeper into the money, early exercise becomes more likely and the time value of the put decreases. The early exercise premium (American - European) typically increases for deep ITM puts.

questions.question: Which of the following can make early exercise of an American call optimal? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Option being deep in the money

questions.explanation: Only an upcoming dividend can make early exercise of an American call optimal. By exercising just before the ex-dividend date, the holder captures the dividend that would otherwise reduce the stock price.
